skill.name: changelog
skill.description: Maintains IdeaVim release notes by reviewing commits, pull requests, issue links, and release tags, then updating CHANGES.md and build.gradle.kts changeNotes.
skill.summary: Maintain IdeaVim changelog entries and marketplace change notes from repository history.
skill.author: JetBrains

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many shell, URL, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirms the shell and network items are expected for changelog maintenance, while the weak crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown text, examples, and filenames. No evidence found for prompt injection, credential theft, destructive commands, or malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Workflow Requires Trust
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run git and GitHub CLI commands to inspect tags, commits, pull requests, and PR comments. This is appropriate for changelog work, but it can expose repository metadata and depends on the local shell and GitHub authentication context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name git and gh commands in a changelog workflow. The commands are read-oriented and relevant, so the concern is operational exposure rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Changelog Filesystem Modification
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to read CHANGES.md and update both CHANGES.md and build.gradle.kts changeNotes. This is the intended behavior, but users should review diffs because the skill modifies release-facing documentation and marketplace notes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and update instructions are explicit and central to the skill purpose. The risk is bounded to documentation and build metadata, with no evidence of hidden file access.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Tracker Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes hardcoded JetBrains, YouTrack, GitHub, and documentation URLs and asks agents to search official sources for context. These links support changelog references and do not show data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible release, issue, PR, and documentation references. No evidence found that secrets or local files are sent to external endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The high weak-crypto and low reconnaissance matches are false positives from Markdown names, formatting examples, and prose. SKILL.md contains no cryptographic implementation, hash use, host probing, credential harvesting, or system inventory instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the referenced lines shows changelog prose, examples, and marketplace text. The static labels do not match executable crypto or reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell command examples for git and GitHub CLI. They are legitimate for repository review, but marketplace users should understand that the skill expects command execution in a trusted repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are present in fenced and inline instructions. They are not obfuscated and are aligned with the changelog maintenance purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Dependent Research Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to use release pages, issue links, pull request metadata, and web search for official context. This is useful for accurate release notes but requires network access disclosure.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references external release pages and search guidance. The destinations are normal project resources, so the pattern is elevated but not malicious.

content.value_statement: Release notes drift when commits, pull requests, issue links, and marketplace notes are reviewed separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a consistent IdeaVim changelog workflow.
actual_capabilities: Reads the current IdeaVim changelog structure and preserves the newest-first release format.
Uses git tags, commit ranges, merged pull requests, and PR comments to identify release changes.
Classifies entries into Features, Fixes, Changes, and Merged PRs using the project rules.
Filters out dependency updates, test-only changes, documentation-only changes, and internal code changes when appropriate.
Keeps CHANGES.md and build.gradle.kts changeNotes aligned with the same release content.
Converts changelog Markdown into the HTML format expected by JetBrains Marketplace change notes.
limitations: The workflow is tailored to IdeaVim and may not match other project changelog formats.
Full results require local git history and optional GitHub CLI access.
Maintainers still need to review release impact and approve final wording.
Sparse commits or missing tests can limit the detail available for user-facing entries.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release Changelog
use_cases.description: Review commits, tags, and merged pull requests to create a complete IdeaVim release entry.

use_cases.title: Document Community Contributions
use_cases.description: Identify external contributor pull requests and add them to the Merged PRs section with attribution.

use_cases.title: Synchronize Marketplace Notes
use_cases.description: Convert the unreleased changelog section into JetBrains Marketplace changeNotes without losing entries.

prompt_templates.title: Check Changelog Status
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of the current release note state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the top of CHANGES.md and tell me whether the IdeaVim changelog has unreleased entries that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Unreleased Entries
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a focused changelog update from recent repository history.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the changelog workflow to review commits and merged pull requests since the last documented release, then draft user-facing entries for the To Be Released section.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Release Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn unreleased work into a versioned release entry.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare the next IdeaVim release notes from the current To Be Released section, preserving categories, ticket links, contributor PRs, and newest-first ordering.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Marketplace Change Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced synchronization between CHANGES.md and Gradle metadata.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare CHANGES.md with build.gradle.kts changeNotes, identify mismatches, and update the marketplace notes so they match the unreleased changelog content.

output_examples.input: Recent commits added a text object and fixed a visual mode issue.
output_examples.output: A concise To Be Released draft with separate Features and Fixes sections, issue links when available, and user-visible wording.

output_examples.input: A community contributor merged a pull request that refactors an internal command handler.
output_examples.output: A Merged PRs entry that keeps the original PR title and contributor attribution, even when the change is not user-visible.

output_examples.input: The Gradle change notes do not match CHANGES.md.
output_examples.output: A synchronized marketplace note summary that preserves every unreleased changelog entry and uses JetBrains Marketplace formatting.

best_practices: Review the generated diff before merging because the skill edits release-facing documentation.
Provide the last processed commit SHA when using automation to avoid duplicate entries.
Check tests and issue links when commit messages do not explain the user-visible behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not include internal refactors unless they belong in the Merged PRs contributor section.
Do not duplicate the same change across Features, Fixes, and Changes.
Do not summarize marketplace changeNotes differently from the matching CHANGES.md content.

faq.question: What project is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the IdeaVim project and its CHANGES.md and build.gradle.kts release note conventions.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands?
faq.answer: It instructs agents to use git and GitHub CLI commands to inspect repository history and pull requests.

faq.question: Does the skill require network access?
faq.answer: Network access is useful for GitHub pull request data, YouTrack links, JetBrains plugin versions, and official documentation references.

faq.question: Can it update marketplace release notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to convert CHANGES.md entries into the HTML-style changeNotes used by JetBrains Marketplace.

faq.question: Will it include every commit?
faq.answer: No. It excludes dependency updates, test-only changes, documentation-only changes, and internal changes unless project rules say otherwise.

faq.question: Is it safe for public marketplace use?
faq.answer: It is suitable with a medium-risk warning because it uses trusted repository commands, file edits, and external project references.
